title: Nuclei Isolation Protocol Multiome 10X Genomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eno6o","text":"Nuclei were isolated from frozen rat brain tissue for Chromium Next GEM Single Cell Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression assay (10 X Genomics). The protocol allowed us to obtain good quality individual nuclei with intact nuclei membrane. The isolated nuclei were assessed for transposition reaction followed by GEM (Gel beads-in-emulsion) generation and barcoding in order to simultaneously profile epigenetic landscape and gene expression in the same nuclei. The protocol is optimized to prevent wetting failure due to nuclei clogging during GEM generation step and to reduce detection of ambient RNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"21mm9","text":"Procedural guidelines ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8u1eq","text":"Use RNaseZap‱ RNase Decontamination Solution (Cat. no. AM9786) to remove RNase contamination of working station and non-disposable items such as centrifuges and pipettes used during nuclei isolation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":12,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6k3et","text":"Perform all steps on ice if possible unless otherwise noted. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3jb11","text":"Use disposable, RNase-free pipettes tips, and tubes. If possible, use broad tips as per the need to prevent nuclei damage during pipetting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8c4d","text":"High quality nuclei with well-preserved round morphology are optimal for single-cell multiome library preparation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c7qd9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"86F1516E9B7511EEAC160A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}

Thus optimize the protocol before proceeding to GEM (Gel beads-in-emulsion) generation steps.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"68k19","text":"      HB Buffer with IGEPAL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71kic","text":"      ATAC-RSB Buffer (without IGEPAL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"424r4","text":"      Sucrose","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5tp0a","text":"      PBS containing 1% BSA","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fis1","text":"      ATAC permeabilization buffer (IGEPAL + Digitonin)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"elq2q","text":"      Sterile water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ct04","text":"      70% Isopropyl alcohol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5j84","text":"      Dounce Tissue Grinder Set","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"da01s","text":"      70 um Flomi cell strainers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0pjd","text":"      Eppendorf LoBind tubes (2 mL and 1.5 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6j1sk","text":"      Swinging-bucket centrifuge","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3crt1","text":"      Pipettes and tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"40pd2","text":"      Ice","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sample Preparation : Maintain Cold Conditions: Perform all steps on ice or at 4ºC to preserve cell integrity
Homogenization:

Use 450 µL of cold Homogenization buffer (HB buffer) with IGEPAL for each sample. Homogenize the tissue using a 2ml Dounce homogenizer with pestle "A" for 10-12 strokes followed by pestle "B" for 10-12 strokes, ensuring to push down and twist.
Incubation and Filteration:
Centrifugation:
Incubate the homogenate for 2 minutes on ice. Gently mix by pipetting up and down, repeating this process two more times during the incubation period.
Filter the homogenate using a 70 µm Flowmi cell strainer.
Collect the filtered suspension into a pre-chilled 2 mL LoBind tube.
Add 550 µL of HB buffer (without IGEPAL) and mix well before centrifugation.

Centrifuge at 500 RCF for 5 minutes at 4ºC to pellet nuclei. Carefully remove the supernatant, leaving behind approximately 50 µL if the pellet is not clearly visible. Keep the supernatant on ice for later steps, if quality check for nuclei loss is needed.
Sucrose Cushion
Resuspend the nuclei in 1 mL of ATAC-RSB buffer (without IGEPAL).
Carefully underlay with 400 µL of ATAC-RSB buffer containing 1.5 M Sucrose.
Look for the separation of layers and then centrifuge at 5500Xg for 30 minutes at 4ºC.
Nuclei permeabilization
Remove most of the supernatant, being careful not to disturb the pellet. Leave behind 50-200 µLif necessary.
Resuspend the nuclei in 450 µL of ATAC permeabilization buffer and incubate for 1 minute on ice.
Filter the suspension using a 40 µm Flowmi cell strainer into a new 1.5 mL LoBind Eppendorf tube.
Add 550 µL of PBS containing 1% BSA, centrifuge at 500 RCF for 5 minutes at 4ºC, discard the supernatant, and resuspend the nuclei in 300 µL of the same PBS solution + 1%BSA.
After centrifugation, remove the supernatant, leaving 10-20 µL for resuspension with 200 µL of 1X Diluted Nuclei Buffer (PN-2000207, 10X Genomics Kit).
Centrifuge at 350 RCF for 5 minutes at 4ºC.
After a final centrifugation, remove the supernatant, leaving 5-6 µL for resuspension.
Nuclei Counting: Prepare Hoechst Solution: Mix 1 µL of Hoechst stock solution (1 mg/mL in PBS) with 99 uL of PBS.

Counting: Prepare a dilution with 0.5 µL of the resuspended nuclei, 1 µL PBS + Hoechst and 8.5 µL of PBS. Place 10 µL on a counting chamber and count under a microscope, covering nine fields to calculate average and standard deviation. Take images to assess the nuclei status and to keep record. Evaluating the quality of nuclei for single-cell multiome analysis: Nuclei with intact round morphology are regarded as high quality and are optimal for single-cell multiome library preparation. A. Brighfield image of brain nuclei, B. DAPI staining of brain nuclei, C. Merged image of brightfield and DAPI channel.

Calculation: Use the provided formula to determine the total number of nuclei. Total nuclei = (Average nuclei count in fields)* (final resuspension volume of nuclei * 10). 12,000-14,000 nuclei are loaded during GEM generation step in order to obtain a target of 6,000 nuclei for further analysis.
Preparation: All stock solutions should be filtered using a 0.22 µm PVDF filter system. Stock solutions are stable at 4ºC for at least 6 months. %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s
